_notes: v2 (Step 3): adds an 'external' funding column alongside 'internal' (cited to sep2022/progress2025) and 'none'. Computed over departments.json's 21 department goals only — the new white-street-landfill entry has no 'goals' array (it's externalOnly) and is excluded from this goal-level rollup by construction. Regenerate this file if departments.json's fundingLink/dataGap fields change.
unitOfAnalysis: department goal (21 total across 12 department/energy-source groupings)

crossTabCollapsed._notes: Same shape as the Step 2 version, for continuity — 'funded' now = internal + external.

verdict.text: Adding the externally-verified GTA funding moves one goal (transportation-diesel / TR-D-G1) from 'unfunded-but-reported' to 'funded-and-reported' — the pattern's exception count drops from 3 to 2. The core finding is now stronger: of 4 funded goals, 100% were reported; of 17 unfunded goals, only 12% were reported. The two remaining unfunded-but-reported goals (LED streetlamps, Police hybrid Interceptors) are both Duke-Energy-driven or routine-fleet-budget items — the same explanation as before. Notably, one of the three 'funded' goals owes its funding entirely to sources the City's own reporting never mentions: the GTA bus fleet is real, well-documented, and financially substantial ($22.4M+ across five federal/utility/settlement grants since 2016), and the Transit Director has publicly credited the SEP for the largest of these grants — yet none of it appears in either SEP source document. This means the two Progress Reports understate the SEP's demonstrated funding impact, not just its goal-tracking completeness.

citation.publisher: Gillig / City of Greensboro Transit Department
citation.title: Transit Department Awarded $22.4 Million for Battery-Electric Buses, Hybrid Buses and Vehicle Chargers

byDepartmentGrouping.note: Updated Step 3: was 0% funded in the Step 2 rollup.

notablePattern: Natural gas goals (Coliseum, Parks & Rec, Fire — 4 goals total) remain a clean 100% funding/reporting blackout, and are now explicitly flagged `deprioritizedInSource` in departments.json — the 2022 SEP itself deprioritized natural gas, so this gap is a documented choice made visible, not a surprising omission, and should read differently in the UI than the other 15 gapped goals.

goalLevelDetail.note: Has a relatedExternalFinding (White Street Landfill solar) — not counted as funded since it's not this goal's own project, but flagged for UI cross-reference.
